condensed_reasoning:
## Research

The agent investigated US TSA passenger volumes for Apr 27–May 3, 2026 (Mon-Sun week) in the context of a jet fuel crisis caused by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closures.

**Data sources consulted:**
- **TSA.gov** official passenger volume data for 2024, 2025, and 2026 (through Apr 9)
- **News sources via Exa search**: Reuters, BBC, Air Cargo Week, IBTimes, travel industry publications covering airline capacity cuts and fare increases
- **Metaculus API**: Retrieved prior forecasts for related questions (Apr 6-12: ~17.92M, Apr 13-19: ~17.10M)
- **AskNews** search returned 403 rate limit errors (blocked)

**Historical baseline established:**
- 2025 Apr 21-27 (Mon-Sun): **17,950,580** passengers
- 2024 Apr 22-28 (Mon-Sun): **17,178,886** passengers  
- Average late April Mon-Sun week: **~17.56M**

## Key Evidence

**Positive signals for passenger volume:**
- Early April 2026 TSA data (Apr 1-9) showed **+1.69%** vs 2025 DOW-matched — surprising resilience despite crisis
- Late April typically shows **+4% seasonal uplift** vs mid-April (2025 pattern)
- US has domestic refining capacity providing buffer vs Europe/Asia

**Negative signals:**
- **Jet fuel prices doubled**: $742 → $1,710/metric tonne
- **United Airlines cutting 5%** of Q2-Q3 capacity; **Delta cutting 3.5%**
- Average fares up **12-18% YoY**
- North American cancellation rate spiked to **14.6%** on one day (vs 4.4% prior year)
- Ryanair CEO warned of 5-10% summer flight cuts if conflict continues into May

**Monte Carlo modeling:**
- Agent built scenario-weighted Monte Carlo with 100K simulations
- Scenarios ranged from "resilient" (+0-3% YoY) at 25% weight to "severe" (-6% to -12%) at 15% weight
- Mixed 70/30 between 2025 and 2024 base values to account for year-to-year variance

## Conclusion

The agent concluded with median estimate of **~17.25M passengers**, representing approximately a 4% decline from the 2025 late April comparable week (17.95M), roughly flat with the 2024/2025 average. The 80% CI spans **16.06M to 18.11M** (~12% width of median).

Key rationale: Early April resilience anchors the forecast upward, but announced capacity cuts (United -5%, Delta -3.5%) and higher fares create downward pressure. Late April seasonal uplift partially offsets additional crisis deepening. The estimate is internally consistent with the agent's prior forecasts for earlier April weeks.

capability_gaps: Structured airline schedule/capacity data API would help quantify announced cuts
Real-time flight cancellation aggregator would provide better crisis metrics than single-day anecdotes
subtle_bugs: Parent question post_id 43112 failed to retrieve but didn't block forecast — unclear why

classification.approach: Monte Carlo simulation with scenario-weighted YoY adjustments applied to DOW-matched historical baseline
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether announced airline capacity cuts (United -5%, Delta -3.5%) will be fully implemented by late April and how much demand will shift to remaining flights

risk_flags.evidence: Agent assigns only 10-15% weight to 'severe' scenario (-6% to -12%) despite Ryanair CEO warning of potential fuel shortages in May. The 14.6% single-day North American cancellation rate suggests tail risk may be underweighted.

Agent notes 14.6% N.Am cancellation rate on one day (3x normal) but early April aggregate shows +1.69% volume growth — this tension between micro disruptions and macro resilience isn't fully reconciled. The high cancellation day may have been followed by reaccommodation catch-up, but this isn't explored.

strengths: Thorough historical baseline establishment with DOW-matching across 2024/2025 — properly accounted for Apr 27 being Monday in 2026 vs Sunday in 2025 vs Saturday in 2024
Good consistency check against own prior forecasts for earlier April weeks (Apr 6-12 at 17.92M partially observed, Apr 13-19 at 17.1M projected)
Monte Carlo simulation with scenario weighting rather than point estimate — appropriately captures crisis uncertainty
Verified all 8 factors against trace evidence in reflection step — no hallucinated claims
Identified seasonal uplift pattern in late April (2025 showed +4% from mid-April) as partial offset to crisis deepening
weaknesses: Did not check prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi) for relevant contracts on airline disruption or fuel crisis outcomes
No attempt to model passenger reaccommodation dynamics — capacity cuts don't translate 1:1 to TSA volume reductions
2024 late April week (17.18M) was 4.3% below 2025 (17.95M) for same DOW pattern — this variance isn't fully incorporated into uncertainty
Could have sought more structured data on actual schedule changes rather than relying on announced percentages

issues: AskNews search_news returned 403 rate limit errors on both calls — capability gap but Exa search compensated adequately
Parent question post_id 43112 failed with 'list index out of range' error but didn't block forecast
